title: Use Forms to edit/update existing items and columns

posts.author: snicho (Stephen Nicholson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Forms are good for creating items, and hopefully this year forms will continue to improve to support more (all?) column types.

It would be great to be able to use forms to view, and also edit, existing items.

For example, we currently have a form set up to allow new employees to create their records for HR purposes. This information is added to an HR board, which is then maintained by the HR manager who adds more details to various additional columns in that board.

When it comes time for prompting an employee to update some of their information (eg. provide details of their new driver’s licence), it is managed via email and then manually updated by the manager.

This workflow could be better managed if we could use a form that is sent to the employee, which just shows selected fields (columns) with their existing information, that could then be updated and submitted.

As an extension of this concept, being able to use a form to view, and step through, all items in a board, and update any records, could potentially improve the UX for users on mobile devices. (This may be implemented in a similar manner to the way that forms work within MSExcel - yes, they are there, but hidden within the menu.)

If this also incorporated filters and customised named forms, then it could be a very powerful feature.

posts.author: IanFury (Ian Fury)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. This would be extremely useful. I would also like the ability to output forms as PDF and workflow via a digital signature platform.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julia-monday.com @brett-monday.com

there is any consideration for this request to include in the dynamic roadmap?

this could be a powerful feature for multipurposes, and as @IanFury says including digital signatures would be awesome!

thanks.

cheers.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
I love this suggestion! I’m not sure if it’s on our roadmap but I will check with @brett-monday.com and see if not if he can suggest this to the domain looking after board views including forms.

In the meantime, one thing did spring to mind which was the forms viewer app although this just allows you to see the form responses in the form view itself but there isn’t the way to make edits from inside the form. Maybe your suggestion could be something added to this app if not the form view itself! I will pass the feedback along!

posts.author: ebrad (Ethan Bradford)
posts.cookedHtml:
Julia,

What is the status on updates or edits to an item via a form?

posts.author: jmich (jeremy michiels)
posts.cookedHtml: Google forms allow the creator to edit their form submissions. This is very useful when using forms for work submissions. I’d like to see Monday add this functionality as change requests are very difficult to manage. It would be very efficient to allow users that submit forms to be able to receive a link that allows them to edit their submission.

posts.author: AnthonytheEngineer (Anthony Gude)
posts.cookedHtml: I am not sure I am understanding, is there a way to edit and update items using forms?

posts.author: trinhtunganh (Harry)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have the same need.
I’m making a customer satisfaction measure system.
In that I have a board for tickets and another board for survey. When item create on 1st board another “linked” item will be created on the 2nd one and I need somehow the form that send to customer could update to the exace-newly created item.

Not sure if anybody have a similar scenario and solution. If yes, please share. Really appreciate it.

posts.author: stevendlc927 (Steven De La Cruz)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a huge oversight in the forms build! we need to be able to update an item with a form. @Julia-monday.com has there been any update on this?

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @stevendlc927
Are you saying that you need to be able to update an existing item with a form submission?

Right now, this isn’t possible but what you could do is use the new import with merge from Excel to achieve the same thing (although a little more round about). This feature is actually also going through some performance changes so if it seems a bit buggy, know that it will change very soon.

Check it out and let me know if it helps?

posts.author: MondayExcelGuru89 (Brandon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Evening

I have also suggested something very similar. The best answer received is as seen below:

Monday Reply:
Form to update an item -this I actually a highly requested feature and we get it every once in a while but at the moment there is no plan on supporting this mainly due to the complexity of development .

Sadly, I think it will be a wile before this will realizes.

posts.author: buccit (Tim Bucciarelli)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes - still very much a need and it would be very helpful:

  1. Allow two types of forms (1) updating form (2) new record form (can’t do both most likely)
  2. For the updating form, existing record (row) data will prepopulate form where available
  3. For the updating form, data entered into a field will replace the existing data in that record
  4. For a new record form, any form submission will create a new record (row) in that board.

Just one idea of an approach.

posts.author: guy.gavish (Guygav)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys

I am glad to introduce to you our new app SuperForm

The app extends the monday form capabilities, SuperForm. Among other features, It supports the people column, connect boards column and allows to UPDATE an existing item directly from a form!

It is also one of the first apps that are supported via monday apps monetization.

Please downlad and try.

Looking forward to get your feedback.

Guy

posts.author: guy.gavish (Guygav)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Hernan,

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi. Nice App.

posts.author: guy.gavish (Guygav)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Hernan,

There are 2 forms types: INSERT or UPDATE

If you select the UPDATE type. then once you click on the form link (which can be protected by a password) the items’ names will be placed as a dropdown. It is searchable with typing.

Please check it out

Guy

posts.author: rowan (Rowan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Guy,

I’m curious how things stand on this request. I see that you mentioned it was planned for version 2 release sometime around August. The two links you included won’t load for me, but looking through the 2022 Release Notes the only updates to the form function I can find are mobile support, signature support, and a few other items. I can’t find anything about using forms to view or update existing items.

My goal in looking for this function is to support the following workflow automations:

  1. Internal staff enter some basic information to open a ticket.
  2. A form is automatically sent to the external contact involved requesting additional information.
    ^ This is the place in the flow where not being able to update an existing item through form entry causes trouble. I want to avoid accidental item duplication as much as possible, but at the same time cutting out manual entry for staff members is also the main goal of using Monday.com.

We would be fine using automations to accomplish this, but at the moment it doesn’t look like there’s an option to automate: On Item creation → search Column X on Board Y for items that match New Item’s Value Z. If found, merge / update Items.

Let me know if I’m missing a way of setting this up and thank you for your assistance!

posts.author: carriegraham (Carrie Graham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any ETA on this feature? I just had this very conversation with your support team and submitted it as a new topic (Sorry, I should have read these more carefully).

Is this a React hook thing? What tools is the form feature built in and how is its GUI being returned / styled? I Wappalyzed your stacks but there’s a lot there, and I’m kind of a newb. If I know what it’s built and styled with, maybe I can help you guys come up with a solution?

In a perfect world, if the columns could just be viewed as interactive fields, as searchable and editable as the table view but with a more form-like GUI, that would be awesome.

In our use-case: the subcomponent contractors just want to interact with the data THEY need, and it’s the side-scrolling that’s getting to them. It would also allow us as an org to take inputs like procurement details, filter that data, and generate sign-offs for clients. That’s in the Needs file, but the Wants file would also allow for more styling than the current form response viewer allows. I love the Logo thing, and the print to PDF is priceless. If it had the option to be styled a bit more, that would be great.

One thing I would consider, instead of creating a form as one view, and creating the viewer as another, I would build both onto a toggle: one being the interactive form which uses input fields to put data into or edit the columns of an exiting item, the other showing the final styled view that the pdf would look like. The PDF publishing view could offer some limited styling choices.

Anyway, that’s the dream

I hope this is considered a useful thing by the majority.

posts.author: palmerijosh (Josh Palmeri)

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
The main limitation here is with anonymous and non-account member forms - there will need to a way to access an item in a form view without being able to log into the account.

Forms for submission generate data and submit to the form engine that adds to the board. However, getting that data to a form is another matter - esp. securely. They will need to implement some sort of opaque token, and a URL for the item, and an entirely new view that exists outside of the authenticated space. There would also need to be something in place to disseminate that URL, either after submission, or via automation.

So there is way more that is part of this than just presenting an item as a form view.

posts.author: vjabbinante13 (Vito Abbinante)
posts.cookedHtml:
I guess there’s confusion, from me anyway, on why app partners have the ability to do this, namely Spot-nik and Superforms, but Monday’s proprietary system can’t do it. Monday built their own form, meaning it’s tied directly to their board and columns.
Superforms just takes a duplicate of all board columns, pulls the existing information from those columns, and pre-fills the form. The admin chooses which fields the user can see, update, not update, etc.

That’s one of my main issues with Monday. It’s a solid platform, but some items that seem like they should be obviously native features are created by app partners, and in some cases Monday says they physically can’t do it themselves. It’s their platform. The apps can’t work unless the platform has the ability for them to do so.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, it can be done. But monday would need to create a view that exists outside the entire existing monday authenticated space for anonymous people to be able to view and edit existing submissions.

posts.author: vjabbinante13 (Vito Abbinante)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does current Monday workform system require whoever is filling out the form to have a Monday account?

If it does, I get why that would be an issue. If it doesn’t matter who fills out the form, as long as the admin of the Monday account gives out the link to the update form itself and the access settings are “anyone with the link” it should be an easy fix. What am I missing?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Thanks for all the great feedback. @Galit-monday.com and the rest of the boards team has been reading over your feedback as they are finishing up the development of the feature to give submitted the ability to update existing items via form.

More information:

  • Submitters that are unidentified or that don’t follow the user & board permissions will be limited to 3 submits per submission per form

  • The alpha release of this feature is expected to start within 2-3 weeks

Due the complexity and sensitivity of the feature, the team is carefully working on feature and are hoping to have it fully released in the next few months.

Thank you for your patiences and make sure to keep an eye out. This feature is coming to your WorkForms soon!

posts.cookedHtml:
As a contracting company We function utilizing boards in a couple different ways. One of them specifically has become vital to the way our workflow is managed and our customer satisfaction is held to our standards.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Osodarck, @Nadi, @Mtorro, @jwlehane, @Jbengston, @Chester, @Arek, @Shadster86, @Carrie, @pfirt, @Jappa, @MK1, @StephBarton, @fatima, @palmerijosh, @MeiraPom @ChrisH, @Karen.Evans, @pinterl @cbcondron

You can add response to existing items (or update them) by collecting answers via Google Forms instead of monday.com forms. Google Forms is a free tool so shouldn’t be an issue here.

We developed an app called Google Forms Integration Plus that allows getting responses from Google Forms directly monday.com.
Google Forms also has advanced capabilities that are missing in monday’s forms, and our app offers more flexible and robust logic. It basically solves all the gaps in monday’s forms.

In this example you can add a response to an existing item by matching the value of the item to the value of one of the answers in the form (like matching item name to a some answer in the form). Here’s an example →

Screenshot 2024-10-11 at 15.15.452174×468 39.7 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - https://monday.com/marketplace/listing/10000616/google-forms-integration-plus

Hope that helps!

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community! Amy here from the monday.com team. We’re excited to announce that the Edit Form Responses feature is now in gradual release for Enterprise users! This highly anticipated update, our #1 most-requested Forms feature, brings a major improvement to how Forms work.

With this update, submitters can now edit their responses after submitting, reducing back-and-forth communication and streamlining workflows. We can’t wait for more users to experience these benefits as the rollout continues.

Making products people love to use means we rely heavily on listening to your feedback, so thank you again for your feedback, it’s truly what drives updates like this!

Hi ! how this could be a solution for a non enterprise users ?
(we are pro users)

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)

posts.author: Osodarck (Kleder Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,
I’d like to know if this feature will only be available for Enterprise plans. Could you please clarify?

posts.author: jstokes (Joanne Stokes)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m excited to see we are moving forward on this feature. However, I think some key features or use cases are missing. I would like to limit which columns someone could edit/update. Example: Person completes form to request approval to spend X dollars to attend a conference. We go through the approval process and they attend the conference. We want a couple of rating columns for them to fill in after they attended. However, I do NOT want them to be able to change the dollar figure they initially requested as that was the amount we approved. And when they complete the original form, I don’t want them to fill in the ratings on something they have yet to experience.

posts.author: Summit22 (Cathleen Arnold)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that this option would be helpful.

Regarding not changing some of the original form columns:

Have you considered a separate board for follow-up form for ratings and then use CONNECT board and column?

posts.author: Jappa (Jasper Schulten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @amybronstein,

Really questions that live with many.

Regards, Jasper

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there Jasper! Thanks for your questions and for sharing your thoughts. Right now, the Edit Form Responses feature is in gradual release for Enterprise users, but we do plan to bring this to Pro users in the future - though we don’t have a specific timeline to share just yet. We will keep you updated as this progresses! As for sending forms for existing items, there isn’t a way to edit them at the moment, but this is something that’s definitely on the roadmap. We appreciate your input - it truly helps shape what we build!

posts.author: mweekend (Matthew)
posts.cookedHtml: Are there details on how to use Edit Form Responses? I am an Enterprise user, but I don’t see where I can do this. Perhaps I have the wrong view selected?

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
You have a ‘Response editing’ option in the form settings page.
Once a form is submitted with the option opted in, a column with the submission link will be added automatically to the board.
Note that at the moment, there is no option to recover that link if you accidently delete it (beside using the activity log obviously).

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would be a great additional feature.

Feature requests

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can do that with SuperForm

monday.com

SuperForm - monday.com Marketplace

Advanced form - updating existing items, subitems and more

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is true; however, you can’t build out a form on Superform as you can with workforms. With workforms, you can create your form on the spot. With Superform, you have to either build the workform first or create all the columns and information, and then update the Superform appropriately. It’s inconvenient.

This would be extremely helpful in processes I’ve created where new items are created on a board that has a form.

For example, employees fill out a request form to go on a site visit. Once approved, a new item will be created on another board for employees to fill out the site visit details (which is also available as a form for easy fill-out). Obviously, Superforms update integration is helpful here. However, this is something I would expect and hope monday would have as well. Monday update links should be available whether the item was created via form or automation.

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Have you tried connecting the two forms together? Once the first one was submitted you can send to the user a link for the second form with the item id of the item that was created from the first form.
Thus connecting both submissions with a connect board column.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s an interesting idea, but there are two issues with that:

  1. The second form is only viable for specific purposes they select in the first form (and there’s no option to direct them to the second form after first form submission based on a choice in the first form).
  2. The second form is only viable after approval. If there’s no approval, there’s no reason for them to fill out the second form.

posts.author: rriker (Rusty Riker)
posts.cookedHtml: What if you merged the 2 boards and then used conditional questions. Using Superform, you could show include the status on the form, but turn off editing. Then you could use the conditions to hide the site visit questions on the initial form submission, and then when the request was approved, email the user with the update link and hide all the initial questions (if necessary) and show the site visit specific questions.

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml: Still relevant.. When approved and other conditions then send link.

posts.author: rriker (Rusty Riker)
posts.cookedHtml: right, but you’d send the superform edit link that was created and then because the status is approved, all the initial questions would be hidden and the approval relevant questions would be shown

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
No doubt,
I’m just offering a decent workaround that doesn’t rely on 3rd party apps.

posts.author: daltonjazz (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: The implementation of this feature does not actually address the core request (made over 5 years ago now). The original request was to give users a way to edit existing items using forms… what we got was a way for users to edit items CREATED with forms. This essentially gives people an ability to edit their own responses (assuming they keep the link handy) but does not give people who manage boards a way to use forms to collect information for already existing items which to my understanding was the core of the original request. Yet again we are directed to third party (paid) solutions for what is reasonable to expect from a core built in function of this platform.

posts.author: Jorgen (Jorgen )
posts.cookedHtml:
Totally agree with you I was actually looking forward to this feature now completely disappointed what has been offered and no longer used it.
I was hoping it was going to solve the large number of problems from moving from small scale to large scale implementation.

posts.author: Rissa1 (Rissa Ream)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that it didn’t answer the ask 100%, as I had that same approach in mind. As always though with monday there are many ways to approach creating a solution that can work for you.
The assumption here is that items are created via a board one at a time. I don’t think this approach works if you are importing items into the board from excel. If the latter is your use case the approach below may not work for you.
To work with what has been developed within monday and avoid using additional apps I have implemented a standard that items are created via a form instead of directly in the table. This way a link is created & I can ensure the columns that are required are filled in and it is hopefully easier for the end user. Then I will either include people in a people column or email column and send a notification out with the link for the details to be updated using the form link. If you need to send to external emails you can integrate your email platform with monday to send notifications to the external email addresses.

Hope this helps.

posts.author: daltonjazz (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: It is an interesting workaround, however if you have automations that pass information from one board to another then it stops working. I have a 3 step process that has an initial information capture form (an approval board), once something is approved an automation creates an item in a planning board where more logistics are added, and then after that item is complete it is moved to a survey board where follow up information is added. My hopes for this feature was that I could create an automation that sends a form to assigned persons to fill in missing information in that 3rd board, bonus points if the automation could prefill the already existing information (item name, date, and a few other key columns) and just prompt the user to fill in the missing information. I don’t think I can accomplish this with your workaround.

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml: You can use pre-fill values and send to the assigned person a URL with the passed information as a GET request, but do mind the information is visible in the URL, and that the person can edit the pre-filled value before submitting the form.

posts.author: jeffhuffman (Jeff Huffman)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having so much fun watching this thread still. We ditched Monday.com for this very deficiency. Y’all should do the same! Monday has always sucked anyway.

posts.author: daltonjazz (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: Right, but unless I’m missing something, there is no URL unless the item was created with a form. You can’t generate URL’s for items created using other methods (automations or just clicking new item) which again means you can only edit form responses, you can’t update other items with this feature.

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml: Or you can simply try OnlyForm app

posts.author: daltonjazz (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: I appreciate that there is a robust 3rd party app development community for Monday.com, but sometimes a feature should be core to the platform and should not require installing a for-pay 3rd party plugin (not allowed by many professional organizations), especially when the exact feature was requested and stated as released, but didn’t actually solve the original ask.

posts.cookedHtml:

That said, I do agree if the edit form, as promised, still isn’t possible within monday, that’s kinda sad.

I read someone just ditched monday because of that

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
I cannot find this supposedly released feature. My use case would be

  1. someone from my team creates an item
  2. a form is send to a customer
  3. the customer fills in information and clicks “send”
  4. the information are filled in the respective columns of the original item

How would I do that?

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you want to use said feature, the item needs to be created from a form, then you can send the generated url to the customer.

another option you can use two boards, the first is where you create the items

the secnod is for form submissions.

when item is created in the 1st one, send the form url with pre-filled item id from the 1st board, then when a form is submitted use a workflow to insert the form data into the item in the 1st board

hope that helps

posts.author: MickaylaM (Mickayla Murray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone, I’m happy to share that Edit Form Responses is officially live for Enterprise users! This allows you to send forms that let people update existing items directly. It’s perfect for things like HR document refreshes or updating specific project fields.

As an update for our Pro accounts, this feature will not be available at this time. However, please know we are always open to hearing your feedback; we constantly review our Pro plan offerings with our senior product teams to ensure we’re delivering the right value.

Your input really does shape these decisions, so please keep the feedback coming. It’s ideas like yours that help us keep the platform evolving. Thanks for helping us make things smoother and simpler!

posts.author: LGill (Lita Gill)
posts.cookedHtml: Having this available for our company would be a workflow and client experience game changer. Our sales team and account managers are constantly exchanging and updating information for a client account. If we had this forms tool with update-edit functionality we wouldn’t have to use other tools instead of Monday. Please consider adding this to the Pro suite!

posts.author: jstokes (Joanne Stokes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Mickayla, we are Enterprise customers. The solution monday.com has provided doesn’t meet the request. We aren’t just asking for someone to update a form they already submitted. We want to be able to send out a form that allows people to update an item on the board - for columns that weren’t on the original form.

The need: Someone submits a form (or we manually add the item to a board). Once it goes through a portion of our process/workflow, we want to send a new form out to them which collects new/additional information in new columns.

An example: Someone submits a request for approval to attend an outside conference (paid for by the company). On the day of the conference, we want to automatically send them an email with a form link questions about the conference (for feedback). We want that information added to the existing item on the board so all data is collected in one place. As a side note, there could even be a step where once the original form is submitted, an email (form link) is sent to their supervisor asking for information/approval/etc regarding that person attending the conference. So, at this point we would have three different forms (original, supervisor, feedback) with different questions all designed to update one item on a board.

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can easily insert data using workflows.

Just send the second form with the item id embedded in the url so you can insert the data in the correct place.

posts.author: rriker (Rusty Riker)
posts.cookedHtml: can you elaborate? what would the string be?

posts.author: jeffhuffman (Jeff Huffman)
posts.cookedHtml: I love keeping an eye on this thread, which continues to affirm my decision to leave Monday.com more than a year ago.
